title: Phyllopodium cordatum
taxonomy: Phyllopodium cordatum is classified as a species within the family Haemodoraceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as bloodworts or quillworts. This family is native to Australia and New Zealand. Phyllopodium cordatum is specifically placed in the genus Phyllopodium, which includes a few species of flowering plants found in Australia. The classification of Phyllopodium cordatum is further refined at the species level, with its full name being Phyllopodium cordatum. Its classification within the Haemodoraceae family and genus Phyllopodium reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in these groups.
uses: Phyllopodium cordatum is traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in South Africa, where it is believed to have various medicinal properties.
